input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

The Windows (WinUI 3) target requires the Windows App SDK, which comes with the VS workload:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want Build Tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# Modify VS to include MAUI + UWP/WinUI components
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## 4. Install Android SDK & JDK

The MAUI workload bundles some Android pieces, but you can also manage them explicitly:

```powershell
# Check what the workload already installed
dotnet android sdk list

# If you need standalone management, install Android CLI tools
winget install Google.AndroidStudio
# — or just the command-line tools: —
# Download from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only

# Set ANDROID_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk",
  "User"
)
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# Accept licenses
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Discover and install required platform versions dynamically
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install the platform your MAUI project targets (check your .csproj)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

For the JDK (MAUI needs JDK 17+):

```powershell
# Check if already present via workload
dotnet android sdk info

# Otherwise install OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Set JAVA_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17*",
  "User"
)
```

## 5. Set Up an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "system-images.*google_apis;x86_64"

# Install an image
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"

# Create an AVD
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" `
  create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

> **Note:** Hardware acceleration (Hyper-V or HAXM) is required. Enable it:
> ```powershell
> # Check Hyper-V status
> Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V
> # Enable if needed
> Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V -All
> ```

## 6. Enable Windows Developer Mode (WinUI target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## 7. Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Discover your target frameworks
Select-String 'TargetFrameworks' *.csproj

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (make sure it's running)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Discover Versions Dynamically (Cheat Sheet)

```powershell
# Latest .NET SDK available
winget show Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --versions

# Installed MAUI workload version
dotnet workload list

# Available Android SDK platforms
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# TFM the project actually targets (to match build commands)
Select-String 'TargetFrameworks' *.csproj

# Windows App SDK version bundled
dotnet nuget list source  # then check NuGet for Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK
```

---

**Key gotchas:** (1) Run PowerShell as **Administrator** for `winget`, Hyper-V, and Developer Mode steps. (2) Restart your terminal after setting environment variables. (3) Match the `android-XX` platform version to what your `.csproj` `TargetFrameworks` specifies.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands right now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
